items.title: The Suns lock in the last of their own priority free agents, finishing negotiations with Williams' Excel Sports Management agents Jeff Schwartz and Jordan Gertler on Thursday night. First young guards Collin Gillespie and Jordan Goodwin over the weekend, now Williams.
items.author: Shams Charania Tweets
items.body_excerpt: The Suns lock in the last of their own priority free agents, finishing negotiations with Williams' Excel Sports Management agents Jeff Schwartz and Jordan Gertler on Thursday night. First young guards Collin Gillespie and Jordan Goodwin over the weekend, now Williams.

media.title: Jordan Goodwin signing solidifies Suns' bench, roster crunch
media.description: The Suns should have one of the league's best benches with the return of Collin Gillespie and Jordan Goodwin, if they can balance their stuffed rotation properly.

items.title: Jordan Goodwin had Early Bird rights with Suns because he was a waiver claim, so start of contract with Lakers counted toward his two years. Deal is for ~exact amount of tax MLE but Suns still have it available; could possibly use it, re-sign Mark Williams, and still juuuust limb
items.author: John Hollinger
items.body_excerpt: Jordan Goodwin had Early Bird rights with Suns because he was a waiver claim, so start of contract with Lakers counted toward his two years. Deal is for ~exact amount of tax MLE but Suns still have it available; could possibly use it, re-sign Mark Williams, and still juuuust limbo under 2nd apron.

items.title: With estimates plugged in for Collin Gillespie and Jordan Goodwin, I've got the Suns projected at $1.2 million over the luxury tax with two open standard roster spots. Phoenix projects to be roughly $6.7M under the first apron.
items.author: KeithSmithNBA
items.body_excerpt:
With estimates plugged in for Collin Gillespie and Jordan Goodwin, I've got the Suns projected at $1.2 million over the luxury tax with two open standard roster spots. Phoenix projects to be roughly $6.7M under the first apron.

Mark Williams is the team's main unsigned FA.

items.title: Jordan Goodwin's deal with the Phoenix Suns will look like this, if it follows standard 8% raise structure:
items.author: KeithSmithNBA
items.body_excerpt:
Jordan Goodwin's deal with the Phoenix Suns will look like this, if it follows standard 8% raise structure:

26-27: $5.8M
27-28: $6.3M
28-29: $6.8M (player option)

Terrific value for the Suns for a solid backup guard. Good for Goodwin too, especially with a player option on the final season.

Goodwin made the Suns roster out of training camp on a non-guaranteed contract and emerged as a key reserve…
